Interpretation: Residual Standard Error (0.3161): This measures the average deviation of the residuals from the regression line. A small value indicates that the residuals are close to zero, which is expected if the Zero Conditional Mean assumption holds.
Multiple R-squared (7.144e-29): This value is effectively zero, meaning that the independent variables explain almost none of the variation in the residuals. This supports the assumption that the residuals are uncorrelated with the predictors.
Adjusted R-squared (-0.000405): The negative value arises because the model’s explanatory power is so low that adding predictors reduces the adjusted R-squared. This further confirms that the predictors do not explain the residuals.
F-statistic (1.764e-25) and p-value (1): The F-statistic tests whether the predictors collectively explain a significant amount of variation in the residuals. The extremely small F-statistic and p-value of 1 indicate that the predictors have no significant relationship with the residuals.

title: ECON 091 Final Project: Cook County Sentencing
text:
5(a) Implications of this model
The model demonstrates that sentence length in Cook County is highly predictable using legally relevant case characteristics such as offense type, charge class, and sentence type, which account for the majority of variation in sentencing outcomes. At the same time, the analysis shows that judicial assignment and defendant demographics remain systematically associated with sentence length even after conditioning on observable case factors. This suggests that sentencing outcomes reflect not only statutory and procedural considerations, but also institutional and demographic patterns that are not fully captured by case-level variables alone.
Importantly, the model’s predictive strength does not imply causal relationships. Rather, it highlights where unexplained variation persists, particularly for longer sentences, which are likely influenced by factors absent from the data such as criminal history, plea negotiations, guideline departures, and case-specific aggravating circumstances. The results therefore underscore both the explanatory power and the limitations of administrative sentencing data.

title: ECON 091 Final Project: Cook County Sentencing
text:
5(b) Recommendations
My reccomendations to the Cook County State’s Attorney’s Office are mainly to look into the disparities in sentencing outcomes based on race. Although this analysis is descriptive and not causal, the possibilty of systematic differences in sentencing by race warrants further investigation. I would recommend conducting more detailed analyses that incorporate additional controls for prior criminal history, plea status, and guideline ranges to better isolate these associations. Additionally, exploring judge assignment mechanisms and their impact on sentencing could help identify potential sources of unwarranted variation. Finally, looking at how this trend has evolved over time is an essential piece in understanding whether policy changes or reforms have influenced sentencing equity.

This class has been a refreshing one: many times I feel that college courses focus too heavily on theory and not enough on practical application. This course struck a great balance between the two, allowing me to both understand the theoretical underpinnings of predictive modeling while also getting hands-on experience implementing models in R. The assignments were challenging but manageable, and I appreciated the opportunity to work with real-world datasets. Overall, I feel much more confident in my ability to conduct data analysis and build predictive models after taking this class.
Even though I have some prior experience with R and statistics, this course helped me solidify my understanding of key concepts, especially in model testing, an essential skill in today’s world. The lectures were clear and well-organized, I also enjoyed the collaborative aspects of the class, such as group test-prep and project reviews, which allowed me to learn from my classmates and see different approaches to problem-solving.
